title: Mausalaparvan - Canto 001
translation_text:
Vaishampayana said: Daruka went to the Kurus and saw the great warriors of the Parthas. He reported the destruction of the Vrishnis in the battle.

Hearing of the destruction of the Vrishnis, the Bhojas, the Kukuras and the Andhakas, the Pandavas, tormented by grief, became frightened in mind.

Then, having taken leave of them, Arjuna, the beloved friend of Keshava, went to see his maternal uncle and said, "This is not so."

Having gone to the abode of the Vrishnis, O Lord, with Daruka, the hero saw Dvaraka like a woman whose lord is dead.

Those who were formerly protected by the Lord of the world, Seeing Partha, the protector, they cried out, "We are without a protector!"

The sixteen thousand women who were Vasudeva's wives made a great noise when they saw Arjuna had come.

Seeing them, O descendant of the Kuru lineage, Arjuna was choked with tears. Deprived of Krishna and his sons, he was unable to look at them.

That city of the Vrishnis and Andhakas, with its horses and fish, its chariots and dust, with its loud sounds of music and chariots, its houses and sacred bathing places, and its great crowds,

with a garland of diamond walls, covered with heaps of jewels and seaweed, with whirlpools of water in the form of streets, and with lakes that are like crossroads.

The great river of Dvārakā, which was held by Rāma and Kṛṣṇa, was like the terrible river Vaitaraṇī, which is held by the noose of time.

The wise Arjuna saw her bereft of the bulls of the Vrishni clan, deprived of glory, joyless, like a lotus in winter.

Seeing Dvārakā and those women of Kṛṣṇa, Partha, emitting a loud cry of tears, fell to the ground.

Then Satyā, Satrājitī, and Rukmiṇī, O lord of the earth, approaching, wept, surrounding Dhananjaya.

Then they rose from the golden seat and sat down, and, surrounding the great-souled one, they stood up.

Then, having praised Govinda and having told the Pandava, and having comforted those women, he went to see his maternal uncle.
shlokas_raw:
वैशंपायन उवाचदारुकोऽपि कुरून्गत्वा दृष्ट्वा पार्थान्महारथान्आचष्ट मौसले वृष्णीनन्योन्येनोपसंहृतान्

श्रुत्वा विनष्टान्वार्ष्णेयान्सभोजकुकुरान्धकान्पाण्डवाः शोकसंतप्ता वित्रस्तमनसोऽभवन्

ततोऽर्जुनस्तानामन्त्र्य केशवस्य प्रियः सखाप्रययौ मातुलं द्रष्टुं नेदमस्तीति चाब्रवीत्

स वृष्णिनिलयं गत्वा दारुकेण सह प्रभोददर्श द्वारकां वीरो मृतनाथामिव स्त्रियम्

याः स्म ता लोकनाथेन नाथवत्यः पुराभवन्तास्त्वनाथास्तदा नाथं पार्थं दृष्ट्वा विचुक्रुशुः

षोडशस्त्रीसहस्राणि वासुदेवपरिग्रहःतासामासीन्महान्नादो दृष्ट्वैवार्जुनमागतम्

तास्तु दृष्ट्वैव कौरव्यो बाष्पेण पिहितोऽर्जुनःहीनाः कृष्णेन पुत्रैश्च नाशकत्सोऽभिवीक्षितुम्

तां स वृष्ण्यन्धकजलां हयमीनां रथोडुपाम्वादित्ररथघोषौघां वेश्मतीर्थमहाग्रहाम्

रत्नशैवलसंघाटां वज्रप्राकारमालिनीम्रथ्यास्रोतोजलावर्तां चत्वरस्तिमितह्रदाम्

रामकृष्णमहाग्राहां द्वारकासरितं तदाकालपाशग्रहां घोरां नदीं वैतरणीमिव

तां ददर्शार्जुनो धीमान्विहीनां वृष्णिपुंगवैःगतश्रियं निरानन्दां पद्मिनीं शिशिरे यथा

तां दृष्ट्वा द्वारकां पार्थस्ताश्च कृष्णस्य योषितःसस्वनं बाष्पमुत्सृज्य निपपात महीतले

सात्राजिती ततः सत्या रुक्मिणी च विशां पतेअभिपत्य प्ररुरुदुः परिवार्य धनंजयम्

ततस्ताः काञ्चने पीठे समुत्थायोपवेश्य चअब्रुवन्त्यो महात्मानं परिवार्योपतस्थिरे

ततः संस्तूय गोविन्दं कथयित्वा च पाण्डवःआश्वास्य ताः स्त्रियश्चापि मातुलं द्रष्टुमभ्यगात्
